title: [lora_l2.c:246] hwid_len bounds check order issue
description: At line 246, the code checks if (size_t)hwid_len > sizeof(hwid), but this happens AFTER the hwinfo_get_device_id() call at line 232. If the driver returns a value larger than sizeof(hwid) due to a bug, buffer overflow has already occurred. The check is too late to prevent corruption. The sizeof(hwid) parameter passed to hwinfo_get_device_id() should be the defense, but the check suggests distrust of the driver. Consider: this check is defensive and the real protection is the sizeof(hwid) limit passed to the function.
close_reason: Fixed: added comment explaining this is defense-in-depth since overflow would have already occurred
